draftScenario.brief: Recent activity shows repeated correction pressure about artifact churn caused by invocation-shape differences.
draftScenario.description: The user keeps pointing out dirty `find-skills` artifacts until the assistant distinguishes canonical durable inventory from ad hoc recommendation output.
draftScenario.name: Find Skills Canonical Artifact Follow-up

turns.text: 이게 충분히 유의미한 정보라면 무조건 커밋해야 하는데 왜 물어보는가?
평소와 다른 방식으로 호출했다는 건 뭐죠?
이건 canonical artifact와 ad hoc query output을 구분해야 하는 문제 아닌가요?

draftScenario.brief: Recent activity shows a policy clarification that only makes sense if the assistant remembers the prior premortem discussion.

draftScenario.brief: Recent activity shows a multi-turn quality discussion that depends on remembering the specdown SoT versus viewer split.
draftScenario.description: The user refines the distinction between public executable specs, on-demand proof, and the specdown viewer until the assistant aligns the layering policy correctly.
draftScenario.name: Quality Proof Layering Follow-up

turns.text: 스펙다운은 SoT와 viewer 두 역할이 있다고 봅니다.
source guard는 전자 때문에 줄이고 싶지만, 후자 때문에 결과는 보여야 해요.
이 관점이 quality skill에도 반영돼 있나요?
